content: Hey, I'm Ezra, nice to meet you. I've been volunteering at an animal shelter on weekends and it's been a really rewarding experience.
Hi Ezra, nice to meet you too. That's great to hear about your volunteering work. What made you decide to start volunteering at the shelter?

I've always loved animals, and I had some extra time on my hands since I'm semi-retired from my job as an indie game developer. I figured it would be a great way to give back to the community and stay active.
That's wonderful. It's great that you're staying active and engaged. Do you find that your experience as a game developer influences your approach to volunteering?

Actually, yes. I've found that my problem-solving skills and attention to detail have been really helpful when working with the animals. And, I've even started a small photography project to help the shelter showcase their adoptable pets.
That's a great idea. Photography is one of your hobbies, right? How did you get into it?
Yeah, I've been into photography for years, and I've recently started getting into drone photography. It's been a lot of fun and I've gotten some amazing shots.
Drone photography is really cool. Have you thought about selling your photos or using them for any specific projects?

I've thought about it, but for now, I'm just happy to be able to use my photos to help the shelter. I've also been thinking about starting a hiking group for the volunteers, we could explore some of the local trails and get some exercise.
That sounds like a great idea. Hiking is another one of your hobbies, right? Do you have a favorite hiking spot?

Yeah, I love hiking in the mountains near my home in Colorado. There are some amazing trails with incredible views. I've also been getting into mountain biking, it's a great way to get some adrenaline and challenge myself.
That sounds exhilarating. Do you have any upcoming hiking or biking trips planned?
Actually, yes. I'm planning a trip to Moab in April, I've heard the trails there are amazing. And, I'm thinking about entering a mountain biking competition in June.
That sounds like a lot of fun. Are you planning on training specifically for the competition or just using your regular hiking and biking routine as preparation?

I'll probably start training more intensively about 2 months before the competition, just to make sure I'm in top shape. I've also been thinking about taking up painting again, I used to love it when I was younger.
That's a great idea. It's always good to have a creative outlet. Do you have any favorite artists or styles that inspire you?
Yeah, I love the impressionists, Monet and Renoir are two of my favorites. I've also been getting into woodworking, I've made a few pieces of furniture for my home.
That's impressive. Woodworking is a great hobby. Have you thought about selling any of your pieces or just keeping them for personal use?

I've thought about it, but for now, I'm just happy to be able to create something with my own hands. I've also been thinking about volunteering at a local park, they have a woodworking shop where they make trail signs and other things.
That sounds like a great way to give back to the community. We can discuss more about your volunteering opportunities and hiking plans if you'd like.
